name: Lina Puah
translated_text:
Sushi San is another Japanese restaurant in the Malim area, and it’s a pleasant place for a relaxed Japanese-style meal. The moment we walked in, the staff were warm, friendly, and welcoming, which made the experience comfortable from the start. They offer a wide variety of Japanese dishes, including many individual set meals, which is great for solo diners or anyone who wants a complete meal without ordering too much.

What really stood out is their salad selection. They probably offer one of the widest ranges of salads among Japanese restaurants in Melaka. The portions are generous, and the salads are filling enough to be enjoyed as a light meal on their own. This is a nice option if you want something lighter or more refreshing.

I had already eaten a bit before dinner, so I didn’t order too many dishes. Still, everything arrived quickly, which I appreciated. Besides the usual green tea, they also serve genmaicha (roasted brown rice tea), which adds a nice Japanese touch.

The restaurant interior has a clean and cosy izakaya-style vibe, making it a comfortable place to hang out with friends or family. Overall, Sushi San offers a pleasant dining experience with good variety, fast service, and a friendly atmosphere. It’s definitely a nice addition to the Japanese food scene in Malim.

I have visited here several times and the food never disappoints me. The cuisines are basically delicious.

What makes me decided to post a review is because of this. I made a last minute reservation a day before a birthday surprise and had also made a special request for the staff to help decorate the private room. I passed my decor stuff and left due to time constraint, and I didn't expect they are so skillful in decors!

They are so helpful. One of the number balloons was burst before we reached the restaurant. The owner called me to ask me whether I have more spare number balloons. I didn't have one. She went out to buy for me 🥺🥺

Thank you Sushi San! Top notch food and team! ❤️❤️

name: Y O
translated_text:
Food is alright, but there are other better options for Japanese cuisine in Melaka.

1. Ramen - Broth tasted alright, not too salty 👍🏻

2. Pork cutlet - Pork was thin for this price. Other restaurants serve thicker slices for this price 😅

3. Grilled chicken - This was the nicest food we had at Sushi San!

4. Pizza - Tasted alright, no wow factor to it

5. Ramen + grilled salmon - Not worth it, salmon was super small for the price charged.

6. Sushi - Sushi rice was under-seasoned? Not sure if it was the day we went only or it does taste like this on any other days.

7. Japanese rice - Very soft and sticky, again, not sure if it was the day we went only or it does taste like this on any other days.

8. Ambience & service - Environment was nice, we were able to have our own space to eat upon request. I appreciate that ☺️ service was considered good too as the waitress did constantly come to check our tea and refilled it for us
